sql-databases.label: SQL and Databases
sql-databases.summary: How SQL interacts with databases and what a database is.

sketch1.title: What is a Database?
sketch1.bodyMarkdown: A **database** is an organized collection of data that can be easily accessed, managed, and updated. Databases store information in a structured way, making it simple to retrieve and manipulate data as needed. Examples include customer lists, product inventories, or student records.

sketch2.title: How SQL Works with Databases
sketch2.bodyMarkdown: **SQL** (Structured Query Language) is a language used to communicate with databases. SQL lets you ask questions about the data, add new information, update existing records, or remove data. Most modern databases use SQL as their main way to interact with the stored information.

sketch3.title: Why Use SQL and Databases?
sketch3.bodyMarkdown: Databases help keep data organized and secure, especially when dealing with large amounts of information. SQL makes it possible to quickly find, change, or analyze data. This is essential for businesses, schools, and many other organizations that rely on accurate and up-to-date information.
